reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (not Tokyo). Tool fetched Akita spots but response is a generic Wikidata dump of shrines and museums — zero cherry blossom signal, no seasonal context, no sakura-tagged spots. The prefecture match is technically correct (Akita is in Tohoku) but constraint on topic (sakura) is completely ignored.
missing_data: Sakura-tagged spots such as Kakunodate weeping cherry trees, Hirosaki Park equivalents in Akita; 'Tohoku' multi-prefecture coverage (Aomori, Iwate, Yamagata, Miyagi also relevant).
improvement_hint: Add category/tag filter to get_spots so queries for '桜' can surface nature/seasonal-tagged items. Alternatively a discover or search_area call with 'sakura' query would better address the intent.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for sake breweries in Niigata. get_local_specialty returned MAFF GI food products (edamame, carrots, lotus root) — not sake breweries. Niigata sake is world-famous (Hakkaisan, Koshi no Kanbai, etc.) but none appear. The data returned is factually accurate and well-structured but completely misses the intent; no sake/brewery items exist in MAFF GI / METI craft datasets for Niigata.
missing_data: Sake breweries and GI-designated sake (越後杜氏 etc.) from Niigata; the tool simply lacks brewery POI data.
improvement_hint: Add a 'sake/brewery' category to get_local_specialty or a dedicated get_breweries tool; alternatively expose a search_area path for '酒蔵' keyword.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts with onsen in Hakuba. Response returns only 6 spots: 1 art museum, 1 botanical garden, and 4 low-quality municipal scrape pages (tourism office pages, not actual ski/onsen spots). Zero ski resorts and zero onsen facilities appear. The municipality filter worked (Hakuba), but the corpus lacks the specific POI types requested.
missing_data: Hakuba 47, Goryu, Cortina, Tsugaike ski resorts; Hakuba Happo-one Onsen, Mimizuku-no-Yu and other onsen near Hakuba.
improvement_hint: Expand Wikidata ingestion to include ski area (Q3558) and onsen (Q625994) entity types for Nagano/Hakuba municipality.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage spots in Kansai (multiple prefectures). Tool fetched only Kyoto, and even those results are generic Wikidata items — kofun, sports grounds, disaster centers — with no foliage/seasonal relevance. Famous momiji spots (Tofuku-ji, Eikan-do, Daigo-ji) do not appear. Single-prefecture scope also misses Nara, Osaka, Hyogo foliage sites.
missing_data: Tofuku-ji, Eikan-do, Arashiyama for Kyoto; Mimuroto-ji for Uji; Nara momiji spots; seasonal/foliage category tagging entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Implement category or tag filter for seasonal attractions (紅葉, 桜) in get_spots; support multi-prefecture queries for regional concepts like 'Kansai'.

reasoning: Arabic query about famous cherry blossom places in Kansai. Tool fetched Nara spots, which is somewhat relevant (Yoshino is world-famous for sakura) but the returned items are kofun, museums, history sites — zero cherry blossom relevance. Yoshino-yama, the most iconic Kansai sakura spot, does not appear. Multi-prefecture Kansai scope also not addressed.
missing_data: 吉野山 (Yoshino), Nara Park, Koriyama Castle cherry blossoms; Osaka's Kema Sakuranomiya for Kansai breadth.
improvement_hint: Same as L2-09: seasonal category filter is the critical missing capability. Yoshino is in Nara so the prefecture is right, but needs sakura-topic filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for dark-sky / stargazing spots on Okinawa's remote islands. Tool returned Okinawa prefecture spots, but all are castles, museums, archaeological sites on the main island — zero remote island coverage (Iriomote, Ishigaki, Yonaguni, Zamami) and no astronomical/stargazing context. The 'remote island' and 'stargazing' constraints are both ignored.
missing_data: Iriomote, Ishigaki, Yonaguni island attractions; Ryukyu-specific dark-sky viewpoints; island ferry/access info for remote islands.
improvement_hint: Ingest remote Okinawan island municipalities (Taketomi-cho, Yonaguni-cho, etc.) into the scrape corpus. Add a tag/category for 'island' and 'nature/outdoor' to enable constraint filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for rural onsen accommodations in Hokkaido. get_hotels returned a hotel list but all are urban properties: ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo Susukino, ANA Crowne Plaza Sapporo, ANA Crowne Plaza Kushiro — city hotels, not rural onsen ryokan. 'Full Circle' and 'Gondola Chalet' near Niseko are ski resort apartments, not onsen inns. Rural onsen (Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Shirogane, Akan) are absent.
missing_data: Ryokan at Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Shirogane Onsen, Akan Lake; rural/onsen type filter would be needed; no onsen designation on hotel records.
improvement_hint: Add 'onsen' boolean or type tag to hotel records sourced from OSM tourism=hotel with hot_spring tags; or add get_onsen tool separate from get_hotels.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about islands to visit in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool fetched Kagawa prefecture spots but returns only mainland museums, parks, and ruins — no actual island POIs. Famous Seto islands (Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Omi-shima) do not appear. The island constraint and the multi-prefecture nature of Seto Inland Sea (also Hiroshima, Ehime, Okayama sides) are missed.
missing_data: Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Omi-shima, Inujima; art island context; ferry route info. Hiroshima/Ehime/Okayama Seto islands absent.
improvement_hint: Ingest island municipality tourism pages (Naoshima-cho, Tonosho-cho for Shodoshima, etc.); add island geography tag to enable filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Response returns 3 METI craft items: Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave (indigo textile), Otani Ware (ceramics), and Awa Washi (paper). The first item is directly on-topic (indigo textile craft), and Awa Washi description mentions indigo dyeing. However, Otani Ware (ceramics) is irrelevant noise. No workshop or experience venue information is provided — only designation facts. Count is sparse (3 items).
missing_data: Actual workshop/experience venue names and addresses; Awa-ai indigo dyeing (藍染) as distinct GI or cultural entry; visitor experience information.
improvement_hint: Separate workshop/experience data from product designation data; add venue-type field to local_specialty items. Otani Ware should not rank above indigo items for this query.

reasoning: German query asks for Buddhist temple lodging (shukubo) on Mt. Koya. get_hotels returned Wakayama hotels but shows only modern city/coastal hotels (HOTEL KII TANABE, Nanki-Shirahama Marriott, Business Hotel Kushimoto) — none on Koya-san. The Koya-san shukubo (temple lodging) category is completely missing from the corpus. The specific location constraint (Koya-san) and accommodation type (shukubo) are both unaddressed.
missing_data: Any of the ~50 Koya-san shukubo temples (Ekoin, Fukuchiin, Kongo Sanmai-in, etc.); Koya-san municipality (Koya-cho) not represented at all.
improvement_hint: Scrape Koya-san Tourism Association pages; add 'shukubo/temple lodging' as a hotel type; ensure Koya-cho (伊都郡高野町) is in the municipality coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area('武家屋敷') returned 89 matches. Top results include Kakunodate-style content from Ishikawa tourism (Nagamachi samurai district in Kanazawa with detailed description) and a Saga page. The Kanazawa result is highly relevant and specific. However, first result is a generic Saga page (off-target), and results include noisy items (Kyoto-related, Chinese/Traditional Chinese language pages). The constraint 'outside Kyoto' is partially respected but not enforced — a Kyoto result appears.
missing_data: Chiran (Kagoshima), Kitsuki (Oita) samurai districts; cleaner filtering excluding Kyoto results.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture exclusion parameter to search_area; improve result ranking to surface most topically relevant spots first over generic prefecture tourism pages.

reasoning: Indonesian query about summer festivals in Akita. get_festivals returned 39 items with Indonesian translations, which is good. However, the constraint 'summer' is not filtered — items include winter festivals (Dainichido Bugaku is a New Year ritual) and items from other prefectures (Kawagoe Hikawa from Saitama, Hayachine Kagura from Iwate). Akita's famous summer festivals (Kanto Festival, Tanabata in Yokote) are not clearly surfaced. No date/season field on most items.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり) explicit date; season filter capability; date field populated for most items.
wrong_data: Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama) and Hayachine Kagura (Iwate) appear in Akita results — wrong prefecture attribution in UNESCO batch.
improvement_hint: Add season/month filter to get_festivals; fix prefecture attribution for multi-prefecture UNESCO items (Hayachine Kagura is Iwate, not Akita).

reasoning: Korean query about small fishing port towns in Sanin region. get_spots for Shimane returned castles, museums, archaeological sites — no fishing port or coastal town POIs. Sanin fishing towns like Hagi, Masuda, Yunotsu, Saigo on Oki Islands do not appear. The 'fishing village' and 'small town' constraints are entirely unaddressed.
missing_data: Hagi (fishing/historic town, also in Yamaguchi/Sanin), Yunotsu (Shimane), Oki Islands fishing culture, Hamada port area.
improvement_hint: Add coastal/fishing village geographic type tagging; scrape fishing port municipality tourism pages in Shimane and Tottori.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi papermaking in Gifu. get_local_specialty (craft) returned 9 items but the visible portion shows ceramics (Mino Ware), lacquer (Hidehira-nuri — actually Iwate, wrong prefecture for production area), and woodwork (Hida Shunkei). Mino Washi (美濃和紙) is a METI-designated craft from Gifu but is buried — not surfaced in the 4000-char truncation. Hidehira-nuri lists production area in Morioka/Hanamaki (Iwate), not Gifu, raising a data concern. Query intent partially served but core answer (washi) is not visible.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) explicitly surfaced; actual workshop venues for hands-on experience.
wrong_data: Hidehira-nuri (craft_id 0502) lists production areas only in Iwate (Morioka, Hanamaki, Oshu, Hiraizumi) with prefecture_codes [03,21]; the Gifu (21) link appears anomalous — Hidehira lacquer is an Iwate specialty.
improvement_hint: Rank results by semantic relevance to query keyword (washi/paper) not just by registration date; fix Hidehira-nuri prefecture attribution.

reasoning: Spanish query about fishing villages on Noto Peninsula. get_spots for Ishikawa returned Kanazawa-area museums, National Crafts Museum, and a few Noto-area items (Wajima Lacquerware Museum, Kitamae ship museums) but these are museums not fishing villages. Noto fishing villages like Sosogi, Wajima morning market area, and Kirara-ga-Hama are absent. The 'Noto Peninsula' location specificity and 'fishing village' type constraint are both missed.
missing_data: Sosogi coast, Wajima fishing port, Tsukumo Bay, Kirara-ga-Hama; specific Noto Peninsula municipality filtering (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho).
improvement_hint: Support municipality-level filtering within prefecture for Noto (e.g., municipality='輪島' or '珠洲'); add coastal/fishing settlement category.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area('砂丘') returned 84 matches but visible results are dominated by Tottori content (Tottori Dune pages, Tottori GI product). One useful item is Tottori Dune shallots (food, irrelevant). Results from Kagawa (Ritsurin Park — unrelated), Saga (general tourism), and Yamagata (general tourism) show poor relevance. No other actual sand dune locations (Sakata in Yamagata, Nakatajima in Shizuoka, Uchihama in Aomori) are surfaced.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka), Sakata Dunes (Yamagata), Uchihama Dunes (Aomori) — the actual lesser-known examples.
improvement_hint: Improve search relevance to surface geographic/natural feature pages over generic prefecture tourism portals; add nature landmark tagging for dune/coastal geography.

reasoning: Chinese query about traditional crafts of the Sanin region. get_local_specialty for Shimane (craft, zh) returned 4 items with high-quality Chinese translations: Iwami Ware, Sekishu Washi, Unshuzorban abacus, and Izumo stone lanterns. All are directly relevant, well-described, with production area details. Note: the query says 山陰 (Sanin = Shimane+Tottori) but only Shimane is queried — Tottori crafts (Tottori pear craft, etc.) are missing. Still, the Shimane results are excellent.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (the other Sanin prefecture); Iwami silverwork if applicable.
improvement_hint: Support multi-prefecture queries or region-level parameter for 'Sanin' to auto-expand to both Shimane and Tottori.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. get_spots returned general Hokkaido wikidata items: archaeological sites, the Ainu Museum, shell mounds, and 'hanafuru' (a flower hub in Eniwa) and Kamifurano Folk Museum. 'hanafuru' in Eniwa is marginally relevant (flower park) and the Kamifurano museum hints at Furano area, but no actual lavender farm POIs appear (Tomita Farm, Nakafurano, etc.) and the 'beyond Furano' constraint is not addressed.
missing_data: Tomita Farm (Nakafurano), Lavender East (Furano area adjacent), Hokusei-no-oka Observation Park (Biei area); 花の拠点はなふる in Eniwa is a useful tangential hit.
improvement_hint: Add agricultural/flower farm POI type to Wikidata ingestion; tag seasonal flower viewing spots; Furano area municipal scrape would surface more relevant items.

reasoning: Filipino query about traditional old towns/heritage towns in Kyushu (translation: 'Mga tradisyonal na bayan ng kape' = traditional coffee towns, though context + 'Kyushu 古い町並み' suggest heritage/old townscape). get_spots for Oita returned mostly museums and archaeological sites, including Nakatsu Castle. The result set has no old townscape POIs and entirely misses other Kyushu prefectures with famous historic towns (Yanagawa, Yame, Usuki, Bungo-Takada in Oita itself).
missing_data: Usuki Stone Buddhas (old town context), Bungo-Takada's Showa-no-machi, Nakatsu old town; broader Kyushu scope needed.
improvement_hint: Add 'historic district / preservation area' category to get_spots; support multi-prefecture regional queries for 'Kyushu'.

reasoning: Japanese query about staying in kominka (traditional old farmhouses) in Hokuriku. get_hotels for Ishikawa returned modern hotels (ANA Crowne Plaza Kanazawa, AB Hotel) and a guest house 'Kanaya'. None are identifiable as kominka stays. The first result is a nameless student housing hostel. Kominka accommodations in Noto or rural Ishikawa (several exist post-earthquake recovery initiatives) are not present. Fukui and Toyama (also Hokuriku) are excluded.
missing_data: Kominka-stay properties in Noto Peninsula or rural Ishikawa; Fukui/Toyama kominka options; no 'machiya' or 'kominka' accommodation type in the hotel schema.
improvement_hint: Add accommodation subtype tags (kominka, machiya, ryokan, shukubo) to hotel records; scrape Hokuriku kominka stay promotion sites.

reasoning: Query asks about mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returned 7 items. First is 'Living with Whales' (maritime culture, off-topic). Second is 'A Treasury of Scenic Beauty: Wakanoura' (coastal scenery). The query is clearly about mountain religion (Koya-san, Kumano Sanzan, Yoshino-Omine pilgrimages) which are the most famous Japan Heritage items from Wakayama. These may appear in items 3-7 (not shown due to truncation), but the ranking puts irrelevant items first.
missing_data: Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range (Japan Heritage #001 or UNESCO) should be ranked first; Yoshino-Omine, Kumano Sanzan.
improvement_hint: Implement semantic re-ranking for Japan Heritage results so pilgrimage/mountain religion items surface above coastal/food culture items when query contains '山岳信仰'.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals on the Sea of Japan coast. get_festivals for Niigata returned 36 items but first results include Dainichido Bugaku (Akita, wrong prefecture), Hayachine Kagura (Iwate, wrong prefecture), Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama — landlocked, not Sea of Japan), and Uozu Tatemon Festival (Toyama). Items from other prefectures appear due to UNESCO multi-region join. No fireworks (花火) festival appears. Niigata's Nagaoka Fireworks Festival — the largest on the Sea of Japan coast — is absent.
missing_data: Nagaoka Fireworks (長岡花火), Kashiwazaki Earthquake Memorial Fireworks; summer season filter; 'fireworks' category entirely missing.
wrong_data: Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama) should not appear in a Niigata query — it is not on the Sea of Japan coast. Hayachine Kagura is Iwate, not Niigata.
improvement_hint: Fix multi-prefecture UNESCO item prefecture attribution; add a fireworks event category; add date/season field to enable summer filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty returned 2 items: Hakata Ori (weaving, partial Oita connection) and Beppu Bamboo Crafts. The Beppu Bamboo Crafts entry is directly on-target with excellent description (material, techniques, production cities in Oita). Hakata Ori is primarily Fukuoka but has Oita prefecture_code legitimately. Both have official METI designation. Minor gap: only 2 items, no workshop/experience venue info, but within the tool's scope this is as good as it gets.
missing_data: Workshop/studio visit information for Beppu bamboo craft artisans; other bamboo-related folk crafts if any exist.
improvement_hint: Add artisan workshop location data to craft items; link production_area_text to get_spots coordinates for craft workshop locations.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. get_spots for Ehime returned kofun, local museums, and a few landmarks (Imabari Castle). Zero cycling route information, no cycling infrastructure POIs, no reference to the famous Shimanami Kaido cycling route which starts in Imabari (Ehime). The cycling constraint is entirely unaddressed. Even POI types that support cycling tourism (scenic roads, rest stops, cycling terminals) are absent.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaido (starts in Imabari, Ehime — directly in the queried prefecture); Iya Valley cycling; cycling rest stop (サイクリングターミナル) POIs.
improvement_hint: Add cycling route / infrastructure category to the spots corpus; Shimanami Kaido specifically should be a high-priority wikidata ingest as it's internationally famous.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returned 183 items but visible results show Nogaku (general), Kibune Matsuri (Kanagawa festival), Okuma Kabuto Festival (Ishikawa) — no kabuki items visible. Kabuki (歌舞伎) as an important intangible cultural property should appear, but regional kabuki venues (Kochi's Kitagawa Mura, Nagano's Ina Valley kagura-kabuki, Niiza-za in various locations) are not surfaced. The 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' constraint is not applied. 183 items without kabuki filter is noise.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues and folk kabuki (地歌舞伎) listings; keyword filter for '歌舞伎' within get_traditional_arts; exclusion of Tokyo/Kyoto results.
improvement_hint: Add category/keyword filter to get_traditional_arts; support exclusion constraints in search_area for prefecture exclusions (not Tokyo/Kyoto).
